channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Arrell Scholarships 2026 in Canada: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity at the University of Guelph for Future Food System Leaders
item.dc:creator: Hadir Arfaoui
item.description:
The Arrell Scholarships 2026 at the Arrell Food Institute offer an exceptional opportunity for ambitious graduate students who are passionate about transforming global food systems. Hosted at the prestigious University of Guelph, this scholarship is designed to support highly talented individuals pursuing a Master’s degree while contributing to innovative, interdisciplinary solutions addressing some of the […]

The post Arrell Scholarships 2026 in Canada: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity at the University of Guelph for Future Food System Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Arrell Scholarships 2026 at the Arrell Food Institute offer an exceptional opportunity for ambitious graduate students who are passionate about transforming global food systems. Hosted at the prestigious University of Guelph, this scholarship is designed to support highly talented individuals pursuing a Master’s degree while contributing to innovative, interdisciplinary solutions addressing some of the world’s most pressing agri-food challenges.

From food security and sustainability to nutrition and economic growth, the Arrell Scholarships empower students to lead meaningful research and collaborate with experts, policymakers, and communities to drive real-world impact.

Why the Arrell Scholarships Stand Out

The Arrell Scholarships are not just financial awards—they are a leadership-driven, research-intensive experience. Selected scholars become part of a dynamic ecosystem where academic excellence meets community engagement and policy influence.

Scholars will work within multidisciplinary teams, collaborating with government bodies, industry leaders, and academic experts to develop innovative solutions in the agri-food sector. This hands-on, impact-oriented approach ensures that recipients gain both academic and practical experience.

Scholarship Value and Duration

The Arrell Scholarships provide generous financial support to ensure students can fully focus on their academic and research goals:

  • $50,000 per year

  • Duration: Up to 2 years (6 semesters)

  • Paid in three equal installments per year

  • Possibility of a one-semester extension (subject to approval)

This funding covers tuition and contributes significantly to living expenses, making it one of the most competitive graduate scholarships in Canada.

Deadline

  • Application Deadline: April 15, 2026

Applicants must ensure that both their graduate program application and the Arrell Scholarship application are fully completed before this date.

Eligibility Criteria

To qualify for the Arrell Scholarships, applicants must:

  • Apply to a Master’s program at the University of Guelph for Fall 2026

  • Have a minimum first-class (A-) academic average

  • Align their research with the mission of the Arrell Food Institute, which includes:

    • Producing healthier and more nutritious food

    • Supporting economic growth

    • Reducing environmental and social impacts of food systems

Applicants may request to defer the scholarship under exceptional circumstances, subject to approval.

Selection Criteria

The selection process is highly competitive and based on a comprehensive evaluation of each candidate. The selection committee assesses:

  • Outstanding academic performance

  • Relevance and innovation of proposed research

  • Alignment with the Arrell Food Institute’s vision

  • Personal motivation and leadership potential

  • Community involvement, including volunteering and knowledge-sharing activities

Application Process: Step-by-Step Guide

Applying for the Arrell Scholarships requires completing two separate but equally important applications:

Step 1: Apply to a Graduate Program

  • Submit a complete application to an eligible graduate program at the University of Guelph

  • Ensure your research proposal aligns with Arrell’s mission (e.g., food security, plant science, nutrition, consumer behavior)

  • Provide all required documents, which may vary by program

  • International students should consult credential equivalency guidelines

Step 2: Submit the Arrell Scholarship Application

A complete scholarship application includes:

  • Statement of research interests and personal motivations (max. 2 pages + 1 page citations)

  • Current CV

  • Unofficial transcripts

  • Reference letter from your proposed supervisor (sent directly via email)

Applicants must ensure both applications are complete before the deadline to be considered.

Important Conditions of the Scholarship

Recipients of the Arrell Scholarships are expected to:

  • Enroll in UNIV*6050 (or an equivalent experiential learning opportunity)

  • Participate in collaborative interdisciplinary projects

  • Maintain full-time enrollment and satisfactory academic progress

Additionally, this scholarship cannot be combined with other major awards such as:

  • Ontario Graduate Scholarships

  • Vanier Scholarships

  • Tri-Agency funding programs

A Unique Opportunity to Shape the Future of Food

The Arrell Scholarships are ideal for students who want to go beyond traditional academic pathways and actively contribute to solving global challenges. With access to cutting-edge research, expert mentorship, and collaborative projects, scholars are equipped to become leaders in sustainable food systems.

If you are passionate about innovation, sustainability, and global impact, this is your chance to study in Canada while making a difference that extends far beyond the classroom.

For more information click here 

Discover more  opportunities here 

The post Arrell Scholarships 2026 in Canada: Fully Funded Master’s Opportunity at the University of Guelph for Future Food System Leaders appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Outdoor Facilitation: Nature & Wilderness Education 2026 in Vietnam — 7-Day Intensive Training in Nature Pedagogy, Sustainability & Experiential Learning
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Outdoor Facilitation: Nature & Wilderness Education 2026 is a transformative 7-day residential training designed for facilitators, educators, and changemakers who want to deepen their connection with nature while learning how to guide others through meaningful outdoor experiences. Hosted in Hue City, Vietnam, this immersive program goes beyond traditional outdoor education by exploring the concept […]

The post Outdoor Facilitation: Nature & Wilderness Education 2026 in Vietnam — 7-Day Intensive Training in Nature Pedagogy, Sustainability & Experiential Learning appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Outdoor Facilitation: Nature & Wilderness Education 2026 is a transformative 7-day residential training designed for facilitators, educators, and changemakers who want to deepen their connection with nature while learning how to guide others through meaningful outdoor experiences.

Hosted in Hue City, Vietnam, this immersive program goes beyond traditional outdoor education by exploring the concept of the ecological self, where identity, belonging, and responsibility are shaped through direct engagement with the natural world.

Participants will gain practical tools to facilitate nature-based learning, design outdoor programs, and integrate sustainability principles into their work, making this an ideal opportunity for those passionate about environmental education, leadership, and experiential learning.

Program Overview

This intensive journey focuses on developing both personal connection with nature and professional facilitation skills.

Participants will explore:

  • How individuals form deep and lasting relationships with nature

  • How natural elements such as landscapes and weather can become active teaching partners

  • How to design safe, inclusive, and reflective outdoor learning experiences

The program emphasizes hands-on learning, reflection, and real-world facilitation practice.

What You Will Learn

Participants will develop skills in the following areas:

Nature Pedagogy and Ecological Self

  • Understanding deep ecology perspectives

  • Exploring identity, belonging, and responsibility through nature

  • Applying relational learning approaches

Nature Connection Practices

  • Sitting spot and sensory awareness techniques

  • Mindful walking and nature journaling

  • Gratitude and reciprocity practices

Place-Based Facilitation

  • Reading landscapes, seasons, and natural cycles

  • Using nature as a co-facilitator

  • Designing immersive outdoor sessions

Facilitation and Leadership Skills

  • Holding reflective and contemplative spaces

  • Practicing co-facilitation with feedback

  • Ensuring inclusivity, consent, and trauma-sensitive learning

Sustainability and Outdoor Ethics

  • Applying Leave No Trace principles

  • Managing water, waste, and low-impact camps

  • Sustainable cooking and fire practices

Program Structure and Daily Flow

The training follows a 7-day, 6-night residential basecamp model, offering a balance between structured learning and flexible adaptation to the natural environment.

Each day typically includes:

  1. Morning nature practices such as mindful walks or sit spots

  2. Group check-ins and reflective sessions

  3. Nature immersion activities and guided exploration

  4. Learning sessions on pedagogy and facilitation

  5. Practice labs with peer feedback

  6. Solo reflection time for journaling and integration

  7. Evening circles with storytelling, ritual, and group sharing

The program is designed to adapt to weather, landscape, and group dynamics, modeling real-life facilitation scenarios.

Location and Dates

  • Location: Hue City, Vietnam

  • Program Duration: April 30, 2026 – May 7, 2026

  • Format: Full-time, in-person residential training

Certification

Participants who complete the program will receive a:

  • Certificate of Continuing Professional Development (CPD)

Additional certification pathways include:

  • Certified Outdoor Youth Facilitator (YEP) through mentored practice

  • International Nature & Outdoor Youth Facilitator & Leader (YEP) upon completing additional training and implementation requirements

Eligibility Criteria

To participate in this program, applicants must:

  • Be 18 years or older

  • Have an interest in nature facilitation, outdoor learning, or environmental education

  • Be willing to engage in quiet reflection and solo experiences

  • Commit to the full program duration

  • Have basic physical fitness for outdoor activities

  • Be comfortable spending extended time outdoors

Facilitators

The program is led by experienced professionals:

  • Dominik Kron – Outdoor guide with experience across Europe and Southeast Asia, specializing in nature-based learning and empowerment

  • Souheang Ly – Outdoor facilitator integrating education, mindfulness, and experiential learning with a focus on sustainability and inclusion

Group Size

  • Minimum: 6 participants

  • Maximum: 12 participants

This ensures a personalized and deeply immersive learning experience.

Safety and Risk Management

The program prioritizes participant safety through:

  • Collection of medical and emergency information

  • Signed consent forms

  • Certified first-aid facilitators

  • Accident insurance coverage (up to USD 5,000)

  • Site-specific risk assessments

The basecamp model ensures a low-risk, stable learning environment.

What Is Included

  • All training sessions and materials

  • Accommodation and campsite fees

  • Accident insurance coverage

  • Daily lunch

  • Dinner ingredients (group cooking)

  • Pre-program online preparation session

What Is Not Included

Participants must arrange:

  • Travel and visa expenses

  • Personal outdoor gear

  • Accommodation before/after the program

  • Optional gear rental (available at extra cost)

  • Breakfast

Scholarship Opportunity

A full scholarship (donor-funded by the German Government) is available for eligible applicants.

  • Eligible Regions: ASEAN and SAARC countries

  • Scholarship Deadline: 19 April 2026

Applicants must submit a separate scholarship application via email.

Application Deadline

  • Program Deadline: 25 April 2026

  • Scholarship Deadline: 19 April 2026

Why You Should Apply

This program is ideal for individuals seeking to:

  • Develop advanced outdoor facilitation skills

  • Deepen their relationship with nature and ecological awareness

  • Learn practical tools for sustainability and environmental education

  • Join a community of like-minded facilitators and changemakers

  • Gain professional certification and mentorship opportunities

How to Apply

To apply for the mentioned scholarship, download the application here and submit it to the email info@yep-academy.org

Click HERE to learn more about the Outdoor Facilitation: Nature & Wilderness Education 2026.

  • Download to Know More What to Pack: Packing List

  • Download to Get to Know the Dos and Don’ts: Code of Conduct

  • Download to Be Aware of Our Policies: Terms and Conditions

For inquiries, contact:
info@yep-academy.org

Discover more other global Conference opportunities on OFY HERE.

The post Outdoor Facilitation: Nature & Wilderness Education 2026 in Vietnam — 7-Day Intensive Training in Nature Pedagogy, Sustainability & Experiential Learning appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Visa Everywhere Pioneers 2026: €60,000+ Grants, Mentorship & Business Support for Refugee Women Entrepreneurs in Europe
item.dc:creator: Emmanuel Mensah
item.description:
The Visa Everywhere Pioneers 2026 programme is now open, offering a transformative opportunity for entrepreneurial women with refugee backgrounds in Europe to scale their businesses, access funding, and gain global recognition. Now in its fourth year, this prestigious initiative celebrates women who are using innovation, creativity, and leadership to drive meaningful impact across industries such […]

The post Visa Everywhere Pioneers 2026: €60,000+ Grants, Mentorship & Business Support for Refugee Women Entrepreneurs in Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Visa Everywhere Pioneers 2026 programme is now open, offering a transformative opportunity for entrepreneurial women with refugee backgrounds in Europe to scale their businesses, access funding, and gain global recognition.

Now in its fourth year, this prestigious initiative celebrates women who are using innovation, creativity, and leadership to drive meaningful impact across industries such as healthcare, education, advocacy, and business. The programme is designed not only to reward outstanding entrepreneurs but also to provide the tools and support needed to accelerate their ventures.

With 30 winners selected for 2026, participants will benefit from financial support, mentorship, and capacity-building opportunities that can significantly enhance their business growth and long-term sustainability.

About the Visa Everywhere Pioneers Programme

The Visa Everywhere Pioneers programme is an initiative that highlights and empowers female entrepreneurs with refugee backgrounds who are making a difference in their communities and beyond.

Over the years, winners have:

  • Launched impactful solutions across multiple sectors

  • Created employment opportunities for others

  • Delivered innovative services to underserved communities

  • Built scalable businesses with measurable social and economic impact

The programme serves as a platform for these entrepreneurs to share their stories, gain visibility, and connect with a wider ecosystem of support.

What Winners Will Receive

Selected winners of the 2026 edition will gain access to a comprehensive support package designed to strengthen both their businesses and personal development.

Financial Support

  • Each winner will receive a €2,000 or £2,000 cash grant (depending on location)

  • A total of over €60,000 in microgrants will be distributed across all winners

Mentorship and Capacity Building

  • Access to tailored business mentorship from industry experts

  • Guidance on scaling operations, strategy, and sustainability

Learning Opportunities

  • Access to language courses to support integration and business growth

  • Exposure to international best practices and entrepreneurial tools

Visibility and Recognition

  • Recognition as a Visa Everywhere Pioneer 2026

  • Opportunity to showcase their business and impact on a global platform

Who Can Apply

The programme is open to female entrepreneurs with refugee backgrounds living in Europe who meet the following criteria:

Basic Eligibility Requirements

Applicants must:

  • Be a woman aged 18 years or older

  • Reside in:

    • Any of the 27 EU Member States, or

    • Switzerland, Norway, Turkey, Iceland, Georgia, Moldova, Ukraine, or the United Kingdom

  • Have a product or service already in the market

  • Have been actively trading for at least 12 months

  • Be registered as a sole trader or business entity

  • Own at least 51% of the business

  • Have a physical or digital presence (e.g., store, website, or platform)

Refugee or Displacement Criteria

Applicants must meet at least one of the following:

  • Have been granted legal refugee status

  • Be an asylum seeker with full work rights

  • Be under international protection or on a humanitarian visa

  • Be from a country listed in the Fragile States Index (High Warning to Very High Alert) and have arrived in their destination country within the past five years

Programme Partners

The Visa Everywhere Pioneers programme is delivered in collaboration with leading organisations that support refugee entrepreneurship:

  • TERN – Supports refugee entrepreneurs in building and scaling businesses

  • SINGA – Promotes inclusion and entrepreneurship for refugees across Europe

  • Odyssea – Provides employability and vocational training for vulnerable groups

  • Delitelabs – Supports newcomers through tailored business and career pathways

These partners provide essential expertise, mentorship, and support systems to ensure the success of participants.

Key Dates and Timeline

Applicants should take note of the following important dates:

  • Applications Open: 17 March 2026

  • Application Deadline: 1 May 2026 (23:59 GMT)

  • Winners Announcement: 20 June 2026 (World Refugee Day)

Applicants are encouraged to submit their applications early to avoid last-minute challenges.

Why You Should Apply

The Visa Everywhere Pioneers programme offers more than just financial support. It is a comprehensive growth platform that enables entrepreneurs to:

  • Strengthen their business strategy and operations

  • Gain access to expert mentorship and industry insights

  • Expand their professional networks across Europe

  • Increase their visibility and credibility

  • Scale their ventures for greater social and economic impact

For women entrepreneurs navigating the challenges of displacement, this programme provides a powerful opportunity to accelerate success and build sustainable businesses.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can apply through the Official Application Platform.

Applicants should ensure they meet all eligibility requirements and prepare strong applications that highlight their business impact, innovation, and growth potential.

Learn more about the Visa Everywhere Pioneers 2026 programme HERE.

Visit OFY HERE to discover more global grant opportunities.

The post Visa Everywhere Pioneers 2026: €60,000+ Grants, Mentorship & Business Support for Refugee Women Entrepreneurs in Europe appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: United Nations UNODC Consultant Vacancy 2026: Intergovernmental Role in New York (Apply Now)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime has announced an exciting consultancy opportunity for professionals interested in international policy, criminal justice, and intergovernmental affairs. This Inter-agency and Intergovernmental Consultant position is based in New York and offers a unique chance to work within the United Nations system, contributing to high-level global discussions and reforms. […]

The post United Nations UNODC Consultant Vacancy 2026: Intergovernmental Role in New York (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime has announced an exciting consultancy opportunity for professionals interested in international policy, criminal justice, and intergovernmental affairs. This Inter-agency and Intergovernmental Consultant position is based in New York and offers a unique chance to work within the United Nations system, contributing to high-level global discussions and reforms.

This opportunity is ideal for individuals with strong analytical, reporting, and policy expertise who want to engage directly with international governance processes and support UNODC’s mission in combating crime, drugs, and related global challenges.

Job Overview

  • Position Title: Inter-agency and Intergovernmental Consultant

  • Job Opening ID: 273319

  • Category & Level: Consultants (CON)

  • Department/Office: United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime

  • Duty Station: New York

  • Date Posted: March 10, 2026

  • Application Deadline: March 24, 2026

  • Contract Duration: April 13 to December 11, 2026

Purpose of the Role

The consultant will play a critical role in strengthening reporting systems and supporting intergovernmental coordination within UNODC. The position focuses on enhancing how information from meetings and events at UN Headquarters is documented, analyzed, and shared across UNODC headquarters and field offices.

Expected Results

By the end of the assignment, the consultant is expected to deliver:

  • Comprehensive analysis of reporting requirements for UN Headquarters events within UNODC’s mandate

  • Improved templates and mechanisms for sharing reports with headquarters and field offices

  • A foresight model to anticipate reporting needs for intergovernmental meetings

  • Monthly analytical reports covering intergovernmental and inter-agency meetings

  • Contributions to reporting related to UN reform processes

These outputs will help streamline communication, improve institutional memory, and enhance UNODC’s strategic engagement in global governance discussions.

Key Duties and Responsibilities

Under the supervision of a Crime Prevention and Criminal Justice Officer, the consultant will:

  • Attend both official and informal meetings of intergovernmental bodies and inter-agency mechanisms

  • Draft detailed analytical reports on meetings held at UN Headquarters, particularly those linked to UNODC’s mandates and UN reform

  • Support the planning and organization of UNODC meetings and events

  • Analyze reporting requirements for UNHQ events relevant to UNODC

  • Develop a foresight model to anticipate reporting needs for intergovernmental engagements

  • Revise reporting templates and improve mechanisms for sharing information, including newsletters

  • Prepare comprehensive reports on intergovernmental and inter-agency discussions

Required Qualifications and Skills

Educational Requirements

  • An advanced university degree (Master’s or equivalent) in:

    • Law

    • Social Sciences

    • Political Science

    • International Relations

    • Criminology

    • Or a related field

  • Alternatively, a bachelor’s degree combined with two additional years of relevant experience may be accepted.

Professional Experience

Applicants should have:

  • Experience working with policy and legal frameworks related to:

    • Drug control

    • Crime prevention

    • Criminal justice

  • Proven experience in drafting analytical reports

  • Experience participating in intergovernmental meetings, including:

    • Secretariat services

    • Negotiations

Language Requirements

  • Fluency in English is required, with strong drafting and communication skills

  • Knowledge of French or any other official UN language is considered an advantage

Work Location and Duration

The consultancy will be based in New York, a global hub for diplomacy and international relations, where the United Nations Headquarters is located.

The contract runs from April 13 to December 11, 2026, offering several months of immersive experience in international policymaking and institutional coordination.

Why You Should Apply

This consultancy provides a rare opportunity to:

  • Work within the United Nations system

  • Gain direct exposure to global governance and UN reform discussions

  • Build expertise in intergovernmental coordination and reporting systems

  • Contribute to impactful work in crime prevention and justice

  • Expand your professional network within international organizations

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by March 24, 2026. Early application is strongly encouraged due to the competitive nature of UN consultancy roles.

APPLY NOW

Important Note

The United Nations does not charge any fees at any stage of the recruitment process. Applicants should be cautious of fraudulent job offers requesting payment.

The Inter-agency and Intergovernmental Consultant role at the United Nations Office on Drugs and Crime is a highly valuable opportunity for professionals passionate about international relations, policy development, and global justice systems. With hands-on involvement in UN processes and reporting mechanisms, this role offers both professional growth and meaningful impact on global governance.

If you meet the qualifications and are eager to contribute to international policy and reform, this is an opportunity worth pursuing.

Discover other similar opportunities here. 

The post United Nations UNODC Consultant Vacancy 2026: Intergovernmental Role in New York (Apply Now) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Commonwealth Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded MA in Human Rights (Distance Learning) at University of London
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for a fully funded MA in Human Rights (Distance Learning) offered by the University of London in partnership with the Commonwealth Scholarship Commission. This opportunity is designed for individuals from eligible Commonwealth countries who are unable to study in the UK due to financial or other constraints. The scholarship supports postgraduate […]

The post Commonwealth Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded MA in Human Rights (Distance Learning) at University of London appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for a fully funded MA in Human Rights (Distance Learning) offered by the University of London in partnership with the Commonwealth Scholarship Commission.

This opportunity is designed for individuals from eligible Commonwealth countries who are unable to study in the UK due to financial or other constraints. The scholarship supports postgraduate study while allowing students to learn remotely from anywhere in the world.

A total of 5 full scholarships are available for the September 2026 intake.

About the MA in Human Rights

The MA in Human Rights is delivered by the School of Advanced Study at the University of London and provides a comprehensive understanding of human rights law, policy, and practice.

The distance learning format makes the program accessible to professionals and students who want to advance their education without relocating. It is particularly suited for individuals already working in advocacy, law, development, or social justice.

The program covers critical issues such as:

Human rights law and policy
Protection of marginalized groups
Environmental justice and global inequalities
Contemporary human rights challenges

Scholarship Benefits

The Commonwealth Scholarship provides full financial support for selected candidates.

Benefits include:

Full tuition coverage for the MA program
Opportunity to study at a globally recognized institution
Flexible distance learning format
Access to academic resources and expert faculty

This scholarship enables students to gain a high-quality postgraduate education without the financial burden of international study.

Who Should Apply

The scholarship is targeted at individuals who are passionate about promoting human rights, particularly for marginalized and underserved communities.

Ideal candidates include those with a demonstrated track record in:

Human rights advocacy
Social justice initiatives
Legal or policy work
Community development

Applicants must show a strong commitment to using their education to create positive change in their communities.

Eligibility Criteria

To be considered for the scholarship, applicants must:

Be from eligible low and middle-income Commonwealth countries
Be unable to study in the UK due to financial or other constraints
Have applied for and received an offer to the MA in Human Rights program
Demonstrate a commitment to promoting human rights

Applicants are encouraged to review the full list of eligible countries before applying.

Important Deadlines

Recommended deadline to apply for the MA programme: March 16, 2026
Scholarship application deadline: March 31, 2026

Applicants are advised to complete their MA application early to ensure eligibility for the scholarship.

How to Apply

Step 1: Apply for admission to the MA in Human Rights programme
https://lnkd.in/eGACGFjy

Step 2: Review eligible countries
https://lnkd.in/eNWJhXYu

Step 3: Apply for the scholarship through the official portal
https://lnkd.in/esKR3eWq

For more Scholarship Opportunities, Visit OFY Here.

The post Commonwealth Scholarship 2026: Fully Funded MA in Human Rights (Distance Learning) at University of London appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Green Financing Opportunities for Women Entrepreneurs in Africa: GIZ Uganda Initiative
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Kampala, Uganda – In a landmark move to empower women-led businesses and accelerate Africa’s transition to a sustainable economy, GIZ Uganda, in partnership with the IYBA WE4A Project and Melanin Kapital, has launched a dedicated green financing initiative targeting women entrepreneurs across multiple African countries. The program is specifically designed to support enterprises that contribute […]

The post Green Financing Opportunities for Women Entrepreneurs in Africa: GIZ Uganda Initiative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Kampala, Uganda – In a landmark move to empower women-led businesses and accelerate Africa’s transition to a sustainable economy, GIZ Uganda, in partnership with the IYBA WE4A Project and Melanin Kapital, has launched a dedicated green financing initiative targeting women entrepreneurs across multiple African countries. The program is specifically designed to support enterprises that contribute to environmental sustainability and climate resilience, providing targeted financial solutions to help these businesses scale effectively.

Expanding Access to Green Finance for Women Entrepreneurs

Women entrepreneurs in Africa continue to face systemic barriers to accessing financing, particularly in sectors related to climate and environmental sustainability. By addressing these challenges, the new program aims to fill a critical funding gap, enabling women-led businesses to thrive while contributing to environmental goals.

The initiative is open to women-led businesses operating in eight African countries: Cameroon, Kenya, Malawi, Mozambique, Senegal, Tanzania, Togo, and Uganda. Through this multi-country approach, the program seeks to maximize the reach of green financing solutions and create a supportive ecosystem for women entrepreneurs across the continent.

Eligible businesses are expected to operate within key green industries, including:

  • Clean technology – such as solar and energy-efficient systems.

  • Renewable energy solutions – including solar, wind, and bioenergy applications.

  • Sustainable agriculture – innovative farming methods that reduce environmental impact.

  • Circular economy solutions – businesses that promote recycling, waste reduction, and sustainable resource use.

  • Eco-friendly manufacturing – products designed with minimal environmental footprint.

These sectors are increasingly recognized as critical to Africa’s sustainable development, yet they often struggle to attract sufficient investment due to perceived risks and limited access to capital. By focusing on these industries, the program aims not only to support women entrepreneurs but also to contribute to broader environmental and climate objectives, including reduced emissions, improved resource efficiency, and enhanced climate adaptation strategies.

Tailored Financial Products for Every Stage of Business Growth

A key component of the initiative is its focus on flexible financing structures designed to meet the needs of businesses at different stages of development. The program offers two distinct financial products:

  1. Green Asset Financing

    • Designed for smaller-scale investments.

    • Loan amounts range from $1,000 to $10,000.

    • Supports the acquisition of green technologies such as solar systems, energy-efficient equipment, and e-mobility solutions.

    • Repayment periods span 9 to 12 months with weekly repayment schedules, offering manageable terms for emerging enterprises.

  2. Working Capital Loan

    • Tailored for more established businesses managing larger operations or orders.

    • Loan amounts range from $50,000 to $85,000.

    • Designed to support inventory purchases, operational expansion, and scaling business activities.

    • Repayment terms are 6 to 9 months, with monthly installments aligned with business cash flow cycles.

Through these offerings, Melanin Kapital ensures that women entrepreneurs—whether just starting or scaling—can access the capital they need without facing restrictive or one-size-fits-all financing conditions.

Eligibility and Application Process

To qualify for financing, applicants must meet the following criteria:

  • Be a registered business with at least six months of operational history.

  • Demonstrate a minimum annual revenue: $5,000 for Green Asset Financing and $10,000 for Working Capital Loans.

  • Show tax compliance and provide verifiable documentation, such as invoices, purchase orders, or financial statements.

The application process operates on a rolling basis, enabling entrepreneurs to apply when their business is ready, rather than adhering to fixed deadlines. This flexible approach ensures that women-led enterprises can access funding at the most strategic point in their growth journey.

APPLY HERE

Driving Africa’s Green Economy Through Women-Led Enterprises

The GIZ Uganda green financing program represents a strategic effort to empower women entrepreneurs while advancing Africa’s green economy. By combining financial support with a focus on environmental impact, the initiative positions itself as a catalyst for inclusive and sustainable business growth across the continent.

Entrepreneurs participating in this program will not only benefit from access to tailored funding but will also join a growing network of innovative and climate-focused enterprises, contributing to a more sustainable and resilient Africa.

As the demand for green solutions continues to rise, programs like this play a crucial role in bridging the financing gap, promoting gender equality in business, and accelerating Africa’s journey toward a low-carbon, environmentally sustainable future.

If you want, I can also create an SEO-optimized meta description and subheadings for this article that will improve its Google search ranking and click-through rate. This will make it perform even better online. Do you want me to do that?

Discover more similar opportunities here. 

The post Green Financing Opportunities for Women Entrepreneurs in Africa: GIZ Uganda Initiative appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Carnegie India Young Ambassadors Program 2026–2027: Paid Policy Opportunity in Technology & Society
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Applications are now open for the Young Ambassadors Program (CIYAP) under the Technology & Society Program at Carnegie India. This one-year, in-person program based in New Delhi, India offers a unique opportunity for recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in research-driven policymaking within a global think tank environment. Running from May 2026 to May 2027, […]

The post Carnegie India Young Ambassadors Program 2026–2027: Paid Policy Opportunity in Technology & Society appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications are now open for the Young Ambassadors Program (CIYAP) under the Technology & Society Program at Carnegie India.

This one-year, in-person program based in New Delhi, India offers a unique opportunity for recent graduates to gain hands-on experience in research-driven policymaking within a global think tank environment.

Running from May 2026 to May 2027, the program is designed to equip future leaders with critical policy skills, research experience, and exposure to global policy conversations.

About the Carnegie India Young Ambassadors Program

The Young Ambassadors Program is tailored for early-career individuals who want to understand how policy is shaped through research and analysis.

Participants will work closely with experts and scholars at Carnegie India, contributing to policy discussions on some of the most pressing global and regional issues.

The program offers an immersive experience in policymaking, allowing participants to engage with multidisciplinary research across areas such as technology, governance, and international affairs.

Program Experience and Learning Opportunities

The selected ambassador will gain practical exposure by working on real-world policy projects and contributing to the work of a globally recognized think tank.

Key experiences include:

Observing and engaging in research-driven policymaking
Collaborating with experts across global Carnegie offices, including Washington D.C., Brussels, Beirut, Berlin, California, and Singapore
Participating in policy discussions and contributing to research outputs
Supporting major events such as the Global Technology Summit and other high-level dialogues
Building professional skills in leadership, analytical thinking, and report writing

Participants will also be mentored by leading scholars and gain valuable insights into how policy decisions are developed and implemented.

Areas of Research

The program focuses on a wide range of technology and policy-related topics.

Applicants may work on areas such as:

Artificial intelligence and emerging technologies
Digital public infrastructure
Cybersecurity and data governance
Space policy and defense innovation
Semiconductors and critical minerals
Nuclear energy and subsea cables

This broad scope allows participants to explore their research interests while contributing to impactful policy work.

Roles and Responsibilities

The selected candidate will support Carnegie India’s research and program activities through a variety of tasks.

Responsibilities may include:

Conducting research and analysis on technology policy topics
Supporting scholars on ongoing research projects
Assisting with writing, editing, and report preparation
Contributing to event organization and coordination
Providing administrative and programmatic support

The role is designed to provide a well-rounded experience across research, communication, and program management.

Benefits of the Program

Participants will receive both professional and financial support during the program.

Benefits include:

Monthly stipend
One-time relocation allowance
Mentorship from leading policy experts
Exposure to global policy networks
Hands-on experience in a leading think tank

This opportunity provides a strong foundation for careers in public policy, research, and international affairs.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

Recent graduate in any discipline
Interest in areas such as technology policy, artificial intelligence, law, economics, or related fields
Ability to work full-time in person in New Delhi

Additional preference is given to applicants from beyond metropolitan cities, as part of the program’s effort to expand access to policy opportunities.

Application Requirements

Interested candidates must submit:

CV
Cover letter
One writing sample

Applications will be evaluated based on merit, without discrimination based on background or identity.

Program Duration

May 2026 – May 2027

How to Apply

Interested applicants should prepare the required documents and submit their application through the official Carnegie India channels.

For more Fellowship oppotunities from OFY, Click here.

The post Carnegie India Young Ambassadors Program 2026–2027: Paid Policy Opportunity in Technology & Society appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Apply Now: UNITAR & Google Free Digital Skills Training 2026 in Selangor (AI and Data Centres)
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
On 13 March 2025 in Geneva, Switzerland, the United Nations Institute for Training and Research officially announced a new call for applications for its flagship skills development initiative titled “Pathway to Prosperity: Upskilling Selangor’s Professionals for a Digital Future (Cohort 2).” This fully funded training programme is designed to empower professionals with in-demand digital skills, […]

The post Apply Now: UNITAR & Google Free Digital Skills Training 2026 in Selangor (AI and Data Centres) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
On 13 March 2025 in Geneva, Switzerland, the United Nations Institute for Training and Research officially announced a new call for applications for its flagship skills development initiative titled “Pathway to Prosperity: Upskilling Selangor’s Professionals for a Digital Future (Cohort 2).” This fully funded training programme is designed to empower professionals with in-demand digital skills, particularly in data centre management and artificial intelligence (AI)—two of the fastest-growing sectors in today’s global economy.

As countries accelerate their digital transformation strategies, regions like Selangor are emerging as critical hubs for innovation, infrastructure development, and tech-driven employment. This programme directly responds to that growth by equipping local talent with practical, job-ready skills aligned with industry needs.

Overview of the Programme

The Pathway to Prosperity programme is a free, structured training course aimed at building technical capacity among early- and mid-career professionals. Delivered through a blend of virtual and in-person learning, the initiative focuses on bridging the skills gap in Malaysia’s digital economy.

Participants will gain foundational and practical knowledge in:

  • Data centre operations and management

  • Artificial intelligence concepts and applications

  • Digital transformation trends and workforce readiness

By combining theoretical learning with hands-on exposure, the programme ensures that learners are not only knowledgeable but also capable of applying their skills in real-world scenarios.

Why This Programme Matters

Selangor plays a pivotal role in Malaysia’s digital growth, hosting a significant share of the country’s data infrastructure and technology investments. As demand for skilled professionals continues to rise, initiatives like this are essential to:

  • Strengthen the local workforce

  • Enhance employability in high-growth sectors

  • Support career transitions into tech-driven roles

  • Align workforce capabilities with national digital priorities

This programme is particularly valuable for individuals looking to future-proof their careers in an increasingly digital job market.

Course Structure and Learning Format

The training will run from 6 April to 28 June 2026, offering a flexible yet comprehensive learning experience. The structure has been carefully designed to accommodate working professionals while maintaining high-quality instruction.

Key Components of the Course

1. Live Online Webinars

  • Held on weekends

  • Duration: Approximately 1.5 hours per week

  • Focused on core concepts, industry insights, and interactive discussions

2. In-Person Workshops

  • Total of three days of face-to-face sessions

  • Scheduled for 13, 27, and 28 June 2026

  • Location: Selangor area

  • Designed for practical application, networking, and hands-on training

3. Certification

  • Participants who successfully complete the programme will receive a joint certification from UNITAR and Google

  • This certification adds significant value to professional credentials and enhances employability

Who Should Apply

One of the most appealing aspects of this programme is its accessibility. It is a fundamental-level course, meaning applicants do not need an advanced technical background to participate.

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  • Be an early- or mid-career professional

  • Be currently based in Selangor, Malaysia

  • Have a strong interest in data centres or artificial intelligence

  • Be motivated to upskill or transition into the digital economy

The programme is inclusive and welcomes a diverse range of participants, including:

  • Employed professionals seeking career advancement

  • Unemployed individuals looking to enter the tech sector

  • Small and medium-sized enterprise (SME) owners

  • Business leaders aiming to integrate digital solutions into their operations

Skills and Benefits You Will Gain

Participants in this programme will benefit from a wide range of professional and practical advantages, including:

Industry-Relevant Skills

You will develop competencies in areas that are highly sought after in today’s job market, particularly in AI and data infrastructure.

Career Advancement Opportunities

With globally recognized certification and hands-on experience, participants can significantly improve their career prospects.

Networking Opportunities

The in-person workshops and live sessions provide opportunities to connect with peers, industry experts, and trainers.

Flexible Learning Format

The combination of weekend webinars and limited in-person sessions makes it easier for working professionals to participate.

No Cost Participation

As a fully funded initiative, the programme removes financial barriers, making high-quality training accessible to a broader audience.

Application Process and Deadline

Interested candidates are required to submit their applications through the official application form provided by UNITAR.

Important Details

  • Application Deadline: 21 March 2026

  • Programme Start Date: 6 April 2026

  • Programme End Date: 28 June 2026

Due to limited available slots, applicants are strongly encouraged to apply as early as possible to increase their chances of selection.

The Pathway to Prosperity: Upskilling Selangor’s Professionals for a Digital Future (Cohort 2) programme represents a strategic opportunity for professionals in Selangor to gain cutting-edge digital skills at no cost. With backing from UNITAR and Google, participants can expect a high-quality learning experience that is both practical and globally relevant.

In a world where digital skills are no longer optional but essential, this programme offers a clear pathway toward career growth, resilience, and long-term success in the digital economy.

Discover more similar opportunities here.

The post Apply Now: UNITAR & Google Free Digital Skills Training 2026 in Selangor (AI and Data Centres) appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: World Bank AgriConnect Course 2026: Free Online Training to Boost Agricultural Jobs and Productivity
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
Enrollment is now open for the AgriConnect course developed by the World Bank Group through its Institute for Economic Development. This online course is designed to equip professionals with practical tools and strategies to improve agricultural productivity and create more jobs across agrifood value chains. As global demand for employment continues to rise, agriculture remains […]

The post World Bank AgriConnect Course 2026: Free Online Training to Boost Agricultural Jobs and Productivity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Enrollment is now open for the AgriConnect course developed by the World Bank Group through its Institute for Economic Development. This online course is designed to equip professionals with practical tools and strategies to improve agricultural productivity and create more jobs across agrifood value chains.

As global demand for employment continues to rise, agriculture remains one of the most important sectors with the potential to absorb labor at scale. However, unlocking this potential requires more efficient, market-driven, and climate-resilient approaches.

This course offers a structured pathway for practitioners to learn how to apply evidence-based strategies to strengthen agricultural systems and drive economic growth.

About the AgriConnect Course

The AgriConnect course is part of a broader learning ecosystem focused on translating global research into actionable strategies for agricultural development.

It is designed for individuals working in development, policymaking, and agribusiness who want to improve outcomes in the agricultural sector.

Through a combination of expert-led content and interactive learning, participants will gain insights into how to design and implement effective agricultural interventions that support productivity, job creation, and income growth.

What You Will Learn

Participants in the course will explore key areas critical to improving agricultural systems and unlocking opportunities across value chains.

The course covers:

How to translate global evidence into practical agricultural development strategies
Approaches to improving irrigation systems and water management
Strategies to enhance market access for farmers and agribusinesses
Methods for building climate-resilient agricultural systems
Tools to improve productivity and efficiency across agrifood value chains

The program combines video lessons, assessments, and peer discussions to ensure a comprehensive learning experience.

Course Benefits

The AgriConnect course offers several benefits for professionals looking to advance their skills in agricultural development.

These include:

Free access to high-quality learning materials
A certificate of completion upon finishing the course
Practical tools and frameworks for real-world application
Opportunities to engage with a global network of practitioners

Participants will leave the course with actionable knowledge that can be applied directly to projects, policies, and business strategies.

Who Should Enroll

This course is ideal for individuals working in or interested in:

International development
Public policy
Agribusiness
Agricultural consulting
Nonprofit and NGO sectors

It is especially valuable for professionals seeking to improve agricultural performance and create sustainable job opportunities.

Course Start Date

Course materials will be available starting March 19, 2026.

Participants can enroll now to gain early access and prepare for the learning experience.

How to Enroll

Interested participants can sign up for the course using the official link .

For more OFY training opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post World Bank AgriConnect Course 2026: Free Online Training to Boost Agricultural Jobs and Productivity appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Australia DFAT Graduate Program 2027: How to Apply, Requirements, Salary and Benefits
item.dc:creator: Hilda Gyenti
item.description:
Applications for the 2027 Graduate Program at the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade are now officially open. This highly competitive program offers graduates the opportunity to launch a rewarding career in international affairs, policy development, corporate management, and global diplomacy. This comprehensive guide explains everything you need to know about the DFAT Graduate Program […]

The post Australia DFAT Graduate Program 2027: How to Apply, Requirements, Salary and Benefits appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
Applications for the 2027 Graduate Program at the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade are now officially open. This highly competitive program offers graduates the opportunity to launch a rewarding career in international affairs, policy development, corporate management, and global diplomacy.

This comprehensive guide explains everything you need to know about the DFAT Graduate Program 2027, including program structure, salary, benefits, eligibility requirements, and career prospects.

Overview of the DFAT Graduate Program 2027

The DFAT Graduate Program is an 18-month, full-time employment program based in Canberra, Australia. It is designed to equip graduates with practical skills, international exposure, and professional experience in government operations and foreign policy.

Key Program Details

  • Organization: Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade

  • Program Duration: 18 months

  • Start Date: February 2027

  • Location: Canberra (relocation support available)

  • Employment Type: Full-time, ongoing APS role

Salary Structure

  • Starting Salary: APS Level 4 – AUD $84,115 + 15.4% superannuation

  • Upon Completion: APS Level 5 – AUD $92,753 + 15.4% superannuation

Program Streams

Applicants can choose between two main streams:

  • Corporate Stream

  • Policy Stream

Working at the Department of Foreign Affairs and Trade (DFAT)

A career with DFAT provides a unique opportunity to contribute to Australia’s global engagement and international strategy. The department plays a critical role in shaping foreign policy, promoting trade, and supporting Australians abroad.

DFAT operates a vast international network of over 116 embassies, consulates, and diplomatic missions across five continents. With more than 6,000 staff worldwide, the organization includes diplomats, analysts, negotiators, and development experts working collaboratively on global challenges.

Key areas of DFAT’s work include:

  • Strengthening international partnerships

  • Expanding trade and investment opportunities

  • Supporting global stability and security

  • Delivering humanitarian and development programs

  • Providing consular assistance to Australians overseas

The department values diversity and actively encourages applications from candidates of varied backgrounds and experiences.

About the Graduate Program Structure

The DFAT Graduate Program is designed to provide hands-on experience through structured rotations and real-world assignments.

Rotational Placements

Participants complete four different placements over the 18-month period. These rotations allow graduates to gain exposure to various functional areas and government priorities.

Corporate Stream: Roles and Career Path

The Corporate Stream focuses on internal operations and organizational management within DFAT.

Possible Placement Areas

  • Consular and crisis management

  • Overseas property management

  • Accounting and financial management

  • Human resources (HR)

  • Diplomatic security

  • Strategic communications

  • Information and communication technology (ICT)

  • Corporate law

  • Australian Passport Office

  • International operations

Career Outcomes

Graduates in the Corporate Stream are well-positioned to apply for overseas postings after completing the program. Potential roles include:

  • Second Secretary

  • Vice Consul (Corporate)

  • Mixed-duty diplomatic positions

Policy Stream: Roles and Career Path

The Policy Stream is ideal for those interested in international relations, diplomacy, and global policy development.

Possible Placement Areas

  • Bilateral economic and political relations

  • International trade negotiations

  • Economic analysis

  • Development and aid program management

  • International security and cyber security

  • International law

  • Public diplomacy

  • Multilateral and global issues

  • Strategic communications

Career Outcomes

Policy graduates may pursue international postings in roles such as:

  • Second Secretary (Policy, Trade, or Development)

  • Diplomatic or mixed-duty roles overseas

Benefits of the DFAT Graduate Program

Participants in the program receive a comprehensive package designed to support professional growth and work-life balance.

What You Will Receive

  • Permanent employment within DFAT

  • Competitive salary progression (APS4 to APS5)

  • 15.4% superannuation

  • Access to training through the Diplomatic Academy

  • Flexible and inclusive work environment

  • Professional networking opportunities

  • Generous leave entitlements

  • Relocation assistance for those moving to Canberra

Who Should Apply?

DFAT is looking for motivated graduates who are passionate about international engagement and public service.

Key Qualities

  • Strong teamwork and collaboration skills

  • Relationship management ability

  • Adaptability and resilience

  • Analytical thinking and problem-solving skills

  • Accountability and initiative

Academic Background Requirements

Corporate Stream Disciplines

Suitable for candidates with backgrounds in:

  • Finance and accounting

  • Business and commerce

  • Human resources

  • Management and marketing

  • Communications

  • Data and ICT

  • Engineering and architecture

Policy Stream Disciplines

Ideal for graduates in:

  • International relations

  • Law

  • Economics

  • Social sciences

  • Public policy

  • History and journalism

  • Linguistics and communication

  • Cyber security and STEM

  • Development and humanitarian studies

Eligibility Requirements

To apply for the DFAT Graduate Program 2027, candidates must meet the following criteria:

  • Be an Australian citizen at the time of application

  • Have completed a bachelor’s degree between 1 January 2022 and 31 December 2026

  • Be eligible to obtain a Negative Vetting Level 2 security clearance

  • Visit here, for eligibility requirement.

Applicants with overseas qualifications must provide official recognition from the Australian education authorities.

Applying from Overseas

Candidates currently living abroad are eligible to apply. However, there are important considerations:

  • Security clearance is mandatory for employment

  • Interviews for clearance must be conducted in Australia

  • Candidates must cover their own travel costs for the security interview

  • Failure to maintain security clearance may result in termination

Security Clearance Requirement

All successful applicants must obtain and maintain a Negative Vetting Level 2 security clearance. This is a critical requirement for working in sensitive government roles and ensures national security standards are upheld.

How to Apply for the DFAT Graduate Program 2027

Applicants must submit their application through the official DFAT careers portal under the “Current Opportunities” section.

Application Deadline

  • Closing Date: Sunday, 12 April 2026

  • Time: 11:30 PM AEST

It is strongly recommended to apply early to avoid last-minute technical issues.

Click here for details on this graduate program.

All queries relating to the application and selection process for the DFAT Graduate Program should be directed to DFP Recruitment via email at dfat.graduates@dfp.com.au

The DFAT Graduate Program 2027 offers a prestigious pathway into one of Australia’s most influential government institutions. With structured training, international exposure, and long-term career prospects, it is an exceptional opportunity for graduates aiming to work in diplomacy, policy, or corporate governance.

Candidates who meet the eligibility requirements and are passionate about contributing to global development and international cooperation should strongly consider applying before the deadline.

Discover more similar opportunities here

The post Australia DFAT Graduate Program 2027: How to Apply, Requirements, Salary and Benefits appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
